interactions.summary: Jiang answers the illiteracy question by saying the story was written for elites and internal consumption, to preserve David's legacy until witnesses died and written truth displaced living memory.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang answering the audience question posed at the end of the previous packet.

speaker_notes.note: The segment is Jiang's answer and closing monologue. No new substantive audience question appears in this focus ref.

claims.claim: Jiang says the apology was written for elites around David, not for the illiterate public, so they would know what story to tell people.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretive claim about the function of early writing stated on 2024-12-05.

claims.claim: He says the written apology preserved David's historical legacy so that, once witnesses to Uriah and Bathsheba were dead, truth would become whatever had been written down.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretive claim about historical memory stated on 2024-12-05.

claims.claim: Jiang reformulates the usual saying as 'history is written by the writers,' not by the winners.
The lecture closes by saying the Hebrew Bible begins as an apology for David but evolves into a much larger mythology.
claims.temporal_scope: Lecture conclusion and forward link stated on 2024-12-05.

signature_moments.moment: When everyone who knows the scandal is dead, truth becomes what is written down.
signature_moments.source_phrase: what is truth now the truth is what's been written down
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It ties writing, time, death, and political memory into the lecture's closing mechanism.

signature_moments.moment: History is not written by the winners; history is written by the writers.
signature_moments.source_phrase: history is not written by the winners history is written by the writers
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This aphorism is the final compressed form of the lecture's argument about text as power.

signature_moments.moment: The Bible starts as David's apology and evolves into a larger mythology.
signature_moments.source_phrase: it starts off as an apology for David but it evolves into a much larger mythology
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This gives the episode its ending and the bridge to the next class.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A political text written for elites inside the regime so they know what story to preserve and repeat.
Jiang's correction to 'history is written by the winners': durable written records, not victory alone, define later truth.

chronology_notes.note: The closing mechanism is temporal: after roughly a generation, when witnesses to Uriah and Bathsheba are dead, the written apology can become the surviving truth.
